conversion_note: v2 seeded conversion of v1 objects/UnitsOfMeasure. v1 discovered two things at load time: (1) UOM_CLASS_REF -- the largest existing UOM class, which resolved to '5' (Quantity); (2) FREE_UOM_CODES -- three unused 3-char UOM_CODE values. Both discovery blocks are DELETED here. The class is now the hard-coded seeded literal '5' (confirmed via read-only BIP 2026-07-20: class 5 is the largest seeded Quantity class, 17 UOMs, none carrying our GldRegUOM prefix -- standard demo-pod data we did not load). The 3-char codes are derived deterministically from the run ${PREFIX} in the runner (${C1}/${C2}/${C3}) so distinct prefixes give distinct codes with no query. UOM_CODE is 3-char capped, so the 5-digit ${PREFIX} rides in the UOM NAME/DESCRIPTION exactly as v1; verification is by name.

package_layout.structure: FLAT CSVs at the zip root plus the manifest (NO nested batch zip). Built by mimicking a live export of INV_MANAGE_UNITS_OF_MEASURE.
members: ASM_SETUP_CSV_METADATA.xml (real, exported live 2026-07-20; ProcessType flipped EXPORT->IMPORT; carries no prefix)
INV_UNIT_OF_MEASURE.csv (the UOMs; ${PREFIX} in name/description, ${C1}/${C2}/${C3} codes, UomClassCode hard-coded '5')
package_layout.csv_format: Header row of column names, comma-separated, every value double-quoted, CRLF line endings.

seeded_reference.note: Hard-coded seeded literal (was v1 discovery UOM_CLASS_REF). Class 5 = Quantity, the largest seeded UOM class on every demo pod. Confirmed via read-only BIP 2026-07-20: 17 seeded UOMs, none carrying our GldRegUOM prefix. Standard demo data we did not load.

code_derivation.note: UOM_CODE is 3-char capped and cannot hold the 5-digit prefix. The runner derives 3 distinct 3-char codes from the prefix deterministically: a 2-char base36 stem = (int(prefix) % 1296) then suffix A/B/C. So distinct prefixes give distinct codes with NO discovery query. The ${PREFIX} rides in the UOM name/description; verification is by name, so a rare code collision does not affect the pass.

good_bad_design.good: Two NEW non-base UOMs placed in the hard-coded seeded class '5' (Quantity). Codes ${C1}/${C2} derived from the prefix; the run ${PREFIX} is carried in the name/description ('GldRegUOM ${PREFIX} A' / 'GldRegUOM ${PREFIX} B'). Standalone reference data, no upstream dependency, no discovery.
good_bad_design.bad: A NEW UOM (code ${C3}) with UomClassCode = 'ZZ_NO_SUCH_CLASS' (a class that does not exist). The Manage Units of Measure CSV import rejects it with 'UomClass does not exist; skipping record' and it never reaches INV_UNITS_OF_MEASURE_B. Deterministic and pod-independent.

verify.note: TL joins to B on UNIT_OF_MEASURE_ID (there is no UOM_ID column). The prefix is in the name.

base_read_good.sql: SELECT b.UOM_CODE AS UOM_CODE, b.UNIT_OF_MEASURE_ID AS UOM_ID, b.UOM_CLASS AS UOM_CLASS, t.UNIT_OF_MEASURE AS NM FROM INV_UNITS_OF_MEASURE_B b JOIN INV_UNITS_OF_MEASURE_TL t ON t.UNIT_OF_MEASURE_ID = b.UNIT_OF_MEASURE_ID WHERE t.LANGUAGE = 'US' AND t.UNIT_OF_MEASURE LIKE 'GldRegUOM ${PREFIX}%' AND t.UNIT_OF_MEASURE NOT LIKE '%BAD%'
base_read_good.expect: 2 rows (A and B) with real UNIT_OF_MEASURE_ID values and UOM_CLASS = 5

bad_absent_read.sql: SELECT UOM_CODE AS C FROM INV_UNITS_OF_MEASURE_B WHERE UOM_CODE = '${BAD_CODE}'
bad_absent_read.expect: zero rows (bad UOM with invalid class rejected, never reaches base)
